ALL SPECIALTIES / DIETITIAN / MISSOURI

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Missouri Dietitian License

Missouri Dietitian license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Dietitian applications in Missouri run through State Committee of Dietitians. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • CDR Registration Card - Copy of your current, active Commission on Dietetic Registration (RD or RDN) credential.
  • Official College Transcripts - Sent from the degree-granting institution showing completion of an ACEND-accredited program.
  • Supervised Practice Verification - Documentation of completed ACEND-accredited dietetic internship hours.
  • Background Check Authorization - Completed authorization for the state background screening process.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 2 to 4 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Missouri does not have a formal reciprocity agreement with other states. Out-of-state dietitians must apply for a new Missouri license through the standard application process. Holding current CDR registration (RD/RDN) satisfies the core requirements. Note: Missouri is actively pursuing the Dietitian Licensure Compact (Senate Bill 412/1339), which would enable interstate practice without additional licenses if enacted.

  1. STEP 1

    Create MoRLD Account and Submit Online Application

    Access the Missouri Board of Healing Arts initial application page and click the 'Apply Online' link to the MoRLD (Missouri Real-time Licensing Database) system. If you're a new user, create an account, then log in and complete the Dietitian application.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Submit Transcripts and Supervised Practice Verification

    Arrange for official transcripts confirming your degree in dietetics or nutrition to be sent directly to the Board. Upload or mail documentation of your supervised practice experience (e.g., Verification Statement from a dietetic internship).

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Provide CDR Examination Verification

    Ensure the Missouri Board of Healing Arts receives verification of your successful completion of the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) examination. This is often done by providing a copy of your CDR registration card within the MoRLD system.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Complete a Criminal Background Check

    Follow the instructions provided within the MoRLD system to complete a fingerprint-based criminal background check through an approved vendor. This typically involves registering with a state-approved service and having your fingerprints taken.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Pay Application Fee

    Submit the non-refundable application fee of $30.00 via the MoRLD online payment system or by mail, as specified in your application.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Board Review and License Issuance

    The Missouri Board of Healing Arts will review your complete application and all supporting documents. Upon approval, your Dietitian license will be issued and mailed to your address on file.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$50
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$50

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
State Committee of Dietitians
Phone
(573) 751-0098
Website
pr.mo.gov/dietitians.asp
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Dietitian renewal in Missouri follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years (by April 1, even years) — no CE required
Renewal fee
$20
Continuing education
See board

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a Missouri dietitian license cost?

The initial application fee is $50. The biennial renewal fee is only $20, due by April 1 of even-numbered years. A $50 late fee applies if renewal is submitted after the 29-day grace period.

Does Missouri require continuing education for dietitians?

No. The State Committee of Dietitians does not mandate continuing education for state license renewal. However, you must maintain your national CDR registration, which requires 75 CPE units every five years.

What is the minimum age to apply for a Missouri dietitian license?

Missouri requires applicants to be at least 21 years old to be eligible for a Licensed Dietitian credential.

Does Missouri have reciprocity for dietitians?

No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Missouri. To work in Missouri, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Missouri?

Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

Can I start working in Missouri while my endorsement application is processing?

Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$50

Processing time

2 to 4 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Not available

National exam

Registration Examination for Dietitians

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Every 2 years (by April 1, even years) — no CE required · $20

Licensing board

State Committee of Dietitians

pr.mo.gov/dietitians.asp

(573) 751-0098
